items.definition_en: A freight train is a train used to carry goods rather than people.
items.example_en: The freight train passed through the city late at night.

items.definition_en: A signal box is a building from which signals and points are controlled on a railway.
items.example_en: The operator worked inside the signal box by the tracks.

items.definition_en: A railway bridge is a bridge built for trains to cross over something.
items.example_en: The train crossed the river on a high railway bridge.

items.definition_en: A level crossing is a place where a road and railway cross at the same level.
items.example_en: Cars waited at the level crossing for the train to pass.

items.definition_en: A railway tunnel is a tunnel built for trains to travel through.
items.example_en: The railway tunnel cuts through the mountain.

items.definition_en: A signal light is a lamp that controls train movements on the railway.
items.example_en: The train stopped in front of the red signal light.

items.definition_en: An overhead line is a system of wires above the tracks to supply power to trains.
items.example_en: The electric train takes power from the overhead line.

items.definition_en: A ticket inspector is someone who checks passengers' tickets on a train.
items.example_en: The ticket inspector walked through the carriage checking tickets.

items.definition_en: A goods wagon is a railway vehicle used for carrying cargo.
items.example_en: The goods wagon was full of fresh produce.

items.definition_en: A train schedule shows the times when trains arrive and depart.
items.example_en: She checked the train schedule before buying her ticket.

items.definition_en: A control room is a place where railway operations are monitored and directed.
items.example_en: Workers in the control room monitored the train movements on their screens.

items.definition_en: First-class is a higher and more comfortable section in a train.
items.example_en: She bought a ticket for first-class to have more space.

items.definition_en: A compartment is a small section inside a train carriage.
items.example_en: They shared a compartment with another family.

items.definition_en: A luggage rack is a shelf for storing bags above the seats on a train.
items.example_en: He put his suitcase on the luggage rack above his seat.

items.definition_en: A train operator is a company that runs trains on a railway.
items.example_en: The train operator announced a delay due to maintenance.

items.definition_en: An automated gate is a mechanical gate at a station that opens with a valid ticket.
items.example_en: Passengers used their tickets to open the automated gate.

items.definition_en: A control panel is a set of controls for operating trains or signals.
items.example_en: The control panel shows the position of all nearby trains.

items.definition_en: A rail junction is a point where two or more railway lines meet or cross.
items.example_en: The train slowed down as it approached the rail junction.

items.definition_en: A departure board is a display showing times and platforms of departing trains.
items.example_en: I checked the departure board for my train’s platform.

items.definition_en: A platform ticket allows someone to enter a station platform without travelling.
items.example_en: She bought a platform ticket to say goodbye to her friend at the train.
